mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below fifty basis points suppresses short-term discount rates and inflates equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of low-rate observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding two hundred thousand signals accelerating labor market collapse that reduces household income expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fifty percent or more of triggered instances when PAYEMS declines exceed 200k month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and increases in unemployment generate a compound negative demand shock that single-series trends miss, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 and UNRATE month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE yields no incremental rank correlation with 60-day returns beyond either series alone, or forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-crisis recovery, housing starts sustained above five hundred thousand units signal stabilization of residential investment and construction employment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with housing market stabilization
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 500, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than fifty percent of observations during post-crisis recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding one percent signals a rapid rebound in manufacturing utilization and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than fifty-five percent of months when INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds one percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in retail sales signals weakening consumer spending that precedes downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in fifty percent or more of instances when RSAFS records a month-over-month contraction.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in the unemployment rate exceeding twenty basis points signals a rapid labor market inflection that reduces recession probability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in forty-five percent or more of instances when UNRATE falls more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates above two percent year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below one percent, negative real interest rates erode equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation scare
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty percent of instances when the joint trigger of CPIAUCSL above 2 percent year-over-year and FEDFUNDS below 1 percent is met, or the interaction adds no marginal explanatory power.
